title: Dipyrena recurvata
intro: Dipyrena recurvata, also known as Borreria clavifolia, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Dipyrena recurvata has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dipyrena recurvata include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dipyrena recurvata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Dipyrena recurvata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Dipyrena.
